posts.text:
Another great example is a few years back when regulations on maritime shipping emissions resulted in reduced cloud seeding.

It's almost unfair to attempt long term climate modeling without also including high resolution political and economic modeling. Everything is connected.

reply_context.text: I think your community is also hamstrung by under-specified models. The problem space is hard enough already, but there's no way climate models could account for things like a significant fraction of the world's oil depots being lit on fire, which surely will have both near and long term impacts.

posts.text: I think your community is also hamstrung by under-specified models. The problem space is hard enough already, but there's no way climate models could account for things like a significant fraction of the world's oil depots being lit on fire, which surely will have both near and long term impacts.

reply_context.text: Definitely. I think the bigger issue here is that we have *nether* at present when it comes to upper tail extremes and historically unseen "rare/weird stuff." We're at the point where we really, truly need to know where we stand in that regard with latest generation models, and we don't.

posts.text: In addition to "phasing" the rhythm of the feed to respect my affect (positive oriented content when I start my day, then present me with news after I'm in the mindset to process it), I want a feed that is appropriately "paced" as well.

posts.text:
I feel like the purpose of conferences rn is less cross-pollination than propagating publish-or-perish culture.

We need more, narrower focused conferences. These exist, but the incentives favor recognizability.

They're at least as much "award show" as "knowledge share".

Maybe I'm just cynical.

reply_context.text:
Yes that's indeed the big big downside
Although I must admit the last big conferences I attended were so crowded that it was paradoxically impossible to meet new people. 700 posters in one session is way too many to have any sort of interaction other than "excuse me, can I go through?"
